data.title: Persuasive Interfaces
data.authors: Rita Orji, Oladapo Oyebode, Alaa Alslaity
data.book: Designing for Usability, Inclusion and Sustainability in Human-Computer Interaction
data.description: Persuasion as a practice is as old as human existence, and it is inherent in human nature. We use persuasion in our daily lives. For example, do you remember a situation where you used different ways to convince your parents to allow you to attend a friend’s party? Or a day when you convinced your friend to accompany you to watch a movie in the cinema? These situations and much more are examples of using persuasion in our daily lives. Whenever people communicate with a clear intended outcome, they are arguably engaging in persuasion (Torning & Oinas-Kukkonen, 2009).
